cards.front: What is frequency domain filtering in image processing?
cards.back: Frequency domain filtering is a technique used to separate an image into its constituent frequencies and then apply filters to remove noise or distortions.

cards.front: What type of noise can be reduced using frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: Frequency domain filtering can reduce periodic noise, such as block noise or stripe noise, that is present in images.

cards.front: What is the main advantage of frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: One of the main advantages of frequency domain filtering is that it can be more effective than spatial domain filtering in removing periodic noise.

cards.front: What is the first step in applying frequency domain filtering to an image?
cards.back: The first step in applying frequency domain filtering to an image is to convert the image into the frequency domain using a Fourier transform or other frequency transformation techniques.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the low-pass filter in frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: The purpose of the low-pass filter in frequency domain filtering is to remove high-frequency components of the image that contain noise, while preserving the low-frequency components that contain useful information.
